title: Solving Absolute Value Equations and Inequalities
curriculumModule: Module 2: Linear Equations, Inequalities and Systems

questions.explanation: $|x| = b$ means $x = b$ or $x = -b$. So $x = 7$ or $x = -7$.
Case 1: $2x - 4 = 10$ gives $x = 7$. Case 2: $2x - 4 = -10$ gives $x = -3$. Both check out.

questions.prompt: What is the solution set of $|3x + 6| + 5 = 2$?
questions.explanation: Isolate: $|3x + 6| = 2 - 5 = -3$. Absolute value can never equal a negative number, so there is no solution.

questions.prompt: For $|ax + b| < c$ (with $c > 0$), the solution is written as which type of compound inequality?
questions.explanation: "Less than" inequalities split into an AND compound inequality: $-c < ax + b < c$ — the solution is sandwiched between two values.
$|2x - 1| \ge 7 \Rightarrow 2x - 1 \ge 7$ or $2x - 1 \le -7 \Rightarrow x \ge 4$ or $x \le -3$. The endpoints are included because of $\ge$.

questions.prompt: Which value is an extraneous solution of $|x - 2| = 3x - 10$?
questions.explanation: Case 2 gives $x - 2 = -(3x - 10) \Rightarrow 4x = 12 \Rightarrow x = 3$. But substituting back: $|3 - 2| = 1$ while $3(3) - 10 = -1$, and $1 \ne -1$ — so $x = 3$ is extraneous. (Case 1 gives $x = 4$, which does check: $|2| = 2 = 3(4) - 10$.)

questions.prompt: What is the solution set of $|5x| = -25$?
questions.explanation: $|5x| = -25$ is impossible because absolute value is always $\ge 0$. The solution set is $\varnothing$.

questions.prompt: Which inequality represents the graph with open circles at $-4$ and $2$ and shading on both outer sides?
questions.explanation: Open circles with shading outside mean a strict "greater than." The center is $\frac{-4 + 2}{2} = -1$ and the distance to each endpoint is $3$, so $|x + 1| > 3$.
